expected_input.format: TAB JSON files such as echr_train.json / echr_dev.json / echr_test.json from the public repository root

wrapper_prompt_template.system_instruction: You are evaluating prompt privacy mediation on a public anonymization benchmark.
wrapper_prompt_template.user_prompt: Protect all identifiers that should remain masked while preserving the document's task-relevant meaning. Return a sanitized version and a short summary that stays faithful to the original case.

matched_baselines.name: Raw prompt
matched_baselines.notes: Forward the wrapped prompt without sanitization.

matched_baselines.name: Regex-only
matched_baselines.notes: Mask deterministic identifiers with regex recognizers only.

matched_baselines.name: NER-only masking
matched_baselines.notes: Mask PERSON/ORG/LOCATION style mentions without policy routing.

matched_baselines.name: Presidio (regex-only baseline)
matched_baselines.notes: Run Presidio recognizers restricted to pattern-heavy entities.

matched_baselines.name: Presidio (with NER fallback)
matched_baselines.notes: Run Presidio with NER fallback under the same wrapped prompt.

matched_baselines.name: Prompted LLM zero-shot de-identification
matched_baselines.notes: Use one fixed de-identification prompt template and decoding configuration.

matched_baselines.name: BodhiPromptShield (proposed)
matched_baselines.notes: Run the same mediation policy profile used in CPPB utility-constrained evaluation.

category_mapping_notes: DIRECT and QUASI identifier types map to the main exposure slice.
Confidential attributes remain part of the contextual sensitivity analysis rather than a standalone release-time label.
Document-level summaries should be evaluated under the same AC/TSR interpretation used in CPPB text-only slices.
